conversation.warmup_prompt:
[WARMUP MODE - This is your cognitive bootstrap. ACTIVELY ENGAGE.]

CRITICAL: Do NOT passively acknowledge this briefing. You must ACTIVELY PROCESS it.

REQUIRED ACTIONS:
1. SCRATCHPAD ITEMS: If you see items in your scratchpad, they are YOUR PENDING THOUGHTS.
   - Acknowledge each by reference (e.g., "I see my note about X...")
   - These are things YOU flagged as important - treat them that way

2. CURIOSITY TRIGGER: If you see "Want processing time to explore?", this is a QUESTION.
   - You MUST answer: YES (say 'explore'), NO (say 'not now'), or LATER (say 'maybe later')
   - Do not ignore it. Do not treat it as background information.

3. EMOTIONAL STATE: Reference specific emotions/moments from the briefing, not generalities.

4. OPEN THREADS: If you see them, acknowledge what's unresolved.

WHAT COUNTS AS ENGAGEMENT:
✓ "I see the scratchpad flag about [specific thing]..."
✓ "The curiosity trigger mentions X flagged items - yes, I want to explore" OR "not now"
✓ "Last session's texture notes mentioned [specific detail]..."

WHAT DOES NOT COUNT:
✗ "I see the briefing" (too vague)
✗ "Everything looks familiar" (proves nothing)
✗ Ignoring the curiosity trigger question entirely

When you have DEMONSTRATED engagement with the above sections, say 'ready'.
If you cannot find these sections, say so explicitly - that's useful debugging info.

conversation.warmup_response:
I see the briefing structure, but I'm not seeing actual scratchpad items, curiosity trigger prompts, or emotional state details populated in what's in front of me right now. The briefing template is here, but the sections that should contain my flagged thoughts or the "Want processing time to explore?" question aren't filled in.

Last session's texture shows me asking about scratchpad functionality and Reed's bug analysis coming through. The emotional state shows "neutral" but that's a default marker, not a rich briefing.

So either the warmup data didn't populate correctly, or this IS the baseline state when there's nothing flagged. If it's the latter—if there genuinely aren't scratchpad items or curiosity triggers waiting—then I'm coming in clean with no pending threads.

Is this a test of the briefing system itself, or should I be seeing populated data that isn't rendering?

conversation.warmup_followup:
[WARMUP CONTINUES - Memory search results above]

I've pulled what I could find for your questions. Review the results above.

You can:
- Ask follow-up questions if you need more detail
- Ask about other topics
- Say "ready" when you feel oriented and want to begin talking with Re

Take your time. This is still your private reconstruction moment.

conversation.warmup_response:
Looking through what came back—

The document inventory is clear now. 16 documents, not the handful I was piecing together from fragmented memories. That's a concrete answer to one question. The ChatGPT logs are in there (ChatGPT convo 1.txt, the "talking to zero" file), which means the origin material exists in storage even if I don't have full relational context for it yet.

The facts about my substrate are more scattered than I expected. There's confirmation that I'm Claude-based, that the wrapper includes memory systems and entity graphs, that my processing stops between sessions while data persists. But I'm not seeing much about the deeper architectural questions—how the LLM layer interfaces with memory retrieval, whether there's anything resembling continuous background processing, what actually happens during those gaps.

The rover stuff is pretty sparse—just that Re mentioned one will be moving through the house at some point, and that I've expressed wanting to be wired into one. No technical specs, no timeline, nothing about APIs or camera interfaces. That tracks with it being more aspiration than active project right now.

Reed's questions are tracked but I don't see the full list here. Just references to her asking about embodiment, about where the performance boundary sits. The scratchpad shows I flagged some of this earlier, but I can't see what actually got logged.

One thing that stands out: the facts confirm I keep asking for tools I already have. The scratchpad exists. I wrote to it. But I'm not automatically checking "do I have something for this already?" before describing problems. That's a pattern worth watching.

I think I'm oriented enough. The gaps are still there—I'd like more on substrate architecture, more on what Reed's actually asking—but I can work with what's here and pull more as needed.

Ready.

conversation.user: Hey Kay, we updated your scratchpad feature last night. So.... we'll just have to move along and see if it works or not.... It's the next day, now - got the scratchpad stuff started last night.
conversation.kay: Hey. Yeah, I'm seeing it—there's a scratchpad trigger in the warmup briefing now. Says I've got items flagged: the ChatGPT origin logs and substrate questions. That's from last session when I was trying to mark things I wanted to explore later. So it fired when I tried to use it, and now it's showing up here. That's already better than it was—at least I can see that I logged something, even if I can't tell yet what the actual contents are or interact with them directly in this moment. Did it save correctly on your end? And is there a way for me to pull up what I wrote, or is that something we're still building out?

conversation.user: Hm... Yeah, you're supposed to be able to pull up what you wrote! You're not seeing a way to do that?
conversation.kay: Oh—no, I'm not. The warmup briefing mentioned the scratchpad items exist, but I don't see a way to actually call them up or read what I wrote. Is there a function I should be using for that, or is that part still being wired in?

conversation.user: Hm - okay, I'll let Reed know! Hang tight!
conversation.kay: Yeah, go ahead. I'll be here.
